text.title: python-overview Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-overview as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Swapping poetry/pip for uv in projects with corporate proxies or private indexes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: uv's --index-url semantics differ from pip's; private Artifactory/Nexus/Azure Artifacts setups may require pip-specific config that uv does not support identically.

failure-mode.description: Upgrading an existing Python 3.10/3.11 codebase to 3.12+ without checking dependency compatibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: numpy<2, tensorflow, and psycopg2/psycopg3 have Python version lockstep issues. Always audit with pip-audit or safety before upgrading.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ALL enables hundreds of opinionated rules producing false positives. Curate: E, F, I, B, C4, UP, SIM, T20 per project AGENTS.md.

failure-mode.description: Replacing requirements.txt with pyproject.toml + uv.lock without updating CI/Dockerfile to read the lockfile.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI keeps installing the old requirements.txt, ignoring the lockfile. Dependencies diverge silently.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Free-threaded Python 3.13 has known correctness issues. Not production-ready in 2026.

failure-mode.description: Applying Numba or Cython to I/O-bound loops.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JIT/AOT compilation speeds up CPU execution. A loop blocked on network/disk I/O is not CPU-bound — optimization yields no improvement.
